name: Hugging Face MCP Server
description: Hugging Face's official MCP server connecting AI assistants to the Hub. Ships 7 built-in tools (search for models, datasets, Spaces, and papers, plus documentation search) and can dynamically attach community Gradio Spaces as additional tools. Hosted at huggingface.co/mcp with per-user configuration, or runnable locally; open source under MIT.

evidence.value: Hosted endpoint runs on Hugging Face Hub infrastructure with Streamable HTTP transport designed for stateless, scalable operation

api_reliability.methodology: Endpoint stability analysis of the hosted server and underlying Hub APIs

evidence.value: Built-in search tools query the Hub's native search across models, datasets, Spaces, and papers with relevant, current results

search_accuracy.methodology: Relevance assessment of Hub search results for representative ML queries

evidence.value: The 7 built-in tools are thin wrappers over stable Hub APIs and succeed consistently

operation_success_rate.methodology: Operation success testing across built-in tools

evidence.value: Attached Gradio Spaces run as community-maintained apps; they can be slow to cold-start, hit ZeroGPU queues, or fail when the Space author changes or breaks the app

dynamic_tool_reliability.methodology: Reliability testing of dynamically attached Gradio Space tools across popular Spaces

evidence.value: Open-source implementation returns structured errors and supports MCP tool-list-changed notifications when the user's configured tool set changes

error_recovery.methodology: Error handling testing including dynamic tool set changes mid-session

evidence.value: Hosted server authenticates with Hugging Face account credentials/tokens; per-user tool configuration is managed at hf.co/settings/mcp

authentication_security.methodology: Authentication mechanism review for hosted and local deployment modes

evidence.value: Hub supports fine-grained access tokens, but local stdio deployments place tokens in client configuration files

token_exposure_risk.methodology: Token storage and exposure-surface analysis across deployment modes

evidence.value: Fine-grained tokens can restrict Hub access, and built-in tools are read-oriented; however, attached Gradio Spaces execute with whatever inputs the agent supplies

scope_limitation.methodology: Permission scope testing of built-in tools and attached Space tools

evidence.value: Dynamically attached Gradio Spaces are arbitrary community-authored applications; tool descriptions and outputs are untrusted third-party content, creating supply-chain and prompt injection exposure inside the agent loop

third_party_tool_supply_chain.methodology: Supply-chain threat modeling of community Space attachment: untrusted code, mutable tool definitions, and unvetted outputs

evidence.value: Built-in tools are search/read operations with limited blast radius; risk concentrates in what user-attached Spaces are allowed to do with submitted data

unauthorized_action_risk.methodology: Authorization boundary analysis of built-in versus attached tool capabilities

evidence.value: Search queries and tool results flow through the hosted server and into the LLM provider's context; built-in tools touch mostly public Hub content

query_data_exposure.methodology: Data flow analysis of queries and results across the hosted server

evidence.value: No built-in redaction; any sensitive content an agent submits to an attached Space tool leaves the Hugging Face trust boundary

sensitive_data_protection.methodology: Assessment of filtering controls on data submitted to attached tools

evidence.value: Org access controls and gated/private repos apply to authenticated Hub access; MCP tool configuration is per-user rather than org-governed

organization_data_control.methodology: Access control review of Hub permissions as applied through the MCP server

evidence.value: Inputs sent to attached Gradio Spaces are processed by community-operated applications whose data handling is not governed by Hugging Face's privacy commitments

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Analysis of data sharing with community Space operators and the LLM provider

evidence.value: Detailed engineering blog plus repository README cover architecture, transports (Streamable HTTP and stdio), and setup for hosted and local modes
Users can see and manage exactly which tools and Spaces are attached at hf.co/settings/mcp; tool calls are visible in MCP client logs
Server is fully open source under MIT (approximately 259 stars, latest release v0.3.28 on 2026-07-07); the same code powers the hosted deployment and can be self-hosted

open_source_transparency.methodology: Source code review of the published server implementation

evidence.value: The 7 built-in tools are clearly enumerated, but the effective tool surface varies per user depending on which Gradio Spaces are attached

api_coverage_clarity.methodology: Comparison of documented tool surface against per-user dynamic configuration

evidence.value: Hosted mode requires only adding https://huggingface.co/mcp and authenticating; tool selection is point-and-click at hf.co/settings/mcp

ease_of_setup.methodology: Setup complexity assessment for hosted and local modes

evidence.value: Built-in search tools respond quickly; attached Space tools vary widely with Space hardware, cold starts, and GPU queues

api_performance.methodology: Latency observation across built-in and attached tools

evidence.value: Hosted endpoint tracks Hub availability, which is historically solid; community Space tools are the main reliability variable

reliability.methodology: Uptime analysis of Hub infrastructure versus attached tool availability

evidence.value: Covers Hub discovery (models, datasets, Spaces, papers, docs) and extends to image generation, transcription, and thousands of other capabilities via attachable Gradio Spaces

feature_coverage.methodology: Feature completeness assessment including the dynamic tool extension model

evidence.value: Approximately 259 GitHub stars with active first-party maintenance (121 releases; latest v0.3.28 on 2026-07-07); adoption driven mainly by the hosted endpoint within the large HF user base

strengths: Fully open source (MIT) with the same code powering the hosted endpoint
Strong Hub discovery: models, datasets, Spaces, papers, and documentation search
Dynamic Gradio Space attachment extends the agent with thousands of community capabilities
Per-user tool configuration UI at hf.co/settings/mcp with tool-list-changed support
Flexible deployment: hosted Streamable HTTP or local stdio
Backed by Hugging Face's first-party maintenance and Hub infrastructure
limitations: Attached Gradio Spaces are arbitrary community apps: a third-party tool supply-chain and prompt injection exposure
Data submitted to attached Spaces leaves Hugging Face's privacy boundary
Attached tool reliability varies with Space cold starts, GPU queues, and author changes
Effective tool surface differs per user, complicating organizational review
Local stdio mode stores Hub tokens in client configuration
MCP tool configuration is per-user with no org-level governance controls

metadata.authentication: Hugging Face account / access tokens (fine-grained supported)

research-assistant.notes: Excellent for discovering models, datasets, papers, and Spaces directly from the Hub

data-analysis.notes: Strong for finding datasets and running analysis-oriented Spaces, with variable attached-tool reliability

code-generation.notes: Doc search and model discovery materially improve ML integration code quality

education.notes: Great for teaching ML concepts with live access to models, papers, and demo Spaces

content-creation.notes: Image generation and media Spaces are attachable as tools, though quality and uptime vary by Space

best_for: ML engineers and researchers discovering models, datasets, and papers from an agent
Developers building Hugging Face integrations with live documentation search
Power users extending agents with community Gradio Space capabilities
Teams that want an auditable, self-hostable open-source MCP server
